images.caption: Figure 1:Two Failure Modes of the Tool Prior Paradox.(a) Format Fragility: rollouts are well-formed under greedy decoding (sampling temperatureτ=0\tau{=}0, format reward≈1\approx 1); under temperature sampling within vanilla GRPO (τ=0.7\tau{=}0.7), the policy reverts to the pretrained<tool_code>tag in place of<tool_call>, often drops closing tags, and stops emitting<answer>altogether (fτ≈0.1f_{\tau}{\approx}0.1).(b) Tool Necessity Gap: tool-call count drops to near-zero within77steps while task accuracy oscillates between0.450.45and0.740.74, as the policy converges on the shortcut of skipping tools.

Figure 2:Cross-Model Evidence for the Tool Prior Paradox under Vanilla GRPO.Qwen3-VL-8B (stronger tool prior) explores tool use but collapses on format, while Qwen2.5-VL-7B (weaker tool prior) stays format-perfect yet emits zero tool calls.

Figure 3:Framework Comparison.(a) Sequential Tool Calling: successive turns re-include the full context, accumulating visual-token overhead; a single mis-localized crop (✗) propagates errors with no peer to correct, yielding an error-amplified answer.(b) Parallel Tool Calling (Ours): one main agent dispatchesKKtool calls concurrently toKKindependent sub-agents (shown forK=3K{=}3); mis-localized peers (✗) are outvoted by correct ones (✓), yielding an evidence-aggregated answer.

Figure 4:Training Dynamics across PARA-GRPO Components.Vanilla GRPO (red) stays flat atfτ≈0.13f_{\tau}{\approx}0.13whileκ\kappacollapses to near zero; Exploration Anchoring (orange) liftsfτf_{\tau}but keepsκ\kappamoderate; nFrames Gating (green) pushesκ\kappaoff-chart while leavingfτf_{\tau}low; only the full PARA-GRPO (blue) stabilizes both axes.

header: Appendix ALimitations, Broader Impact, and Future Work

images.caption: Figure 5:End-to-end progressionfrom Qwen3-VL-8B through the cold-started (step500500) checkpoint to PARA-GRPO across three6464f QA-style benchmarks (VideoMME w/o sub, VideoMME w/ sub, LongVideoBench). Numbers above each triplet are the base→\toPARA-GRPO delta. The cold start delivers the bulk of the eval headroom, and RL adds the training-time format and tool-use stability that transfers to deployment-time robustness (Figure˜4).

Figure 6:Format↔\leftrightarroweval correlationacross1010PARA-GRPO checkpoints. Training-time format reward (xx) tracks greedy-eval VideoMME accuracy (yy) at Pearsonr=0.86r{=}0.86(p<0.01p{<}0.01); the square marks the cold-started (step500500) pre-RL anchor.

Figure 7:The Tool Prior Paradox (two-model trajectory).(a)Qwen3-VL’s format climbs from0.130.13to0.410.41under PARA-GRPO; Qwen2.5-VL stays near0.850.85.(b)Qwen3-VL settles at a moderate tool-call rate (κ=0.21\kappa{=}0.21calls per rollout); Qwen2.5-VL emits zero tool calls. ComplementsFigure˜2.

Figure 8:Training-time tool usage(rollout averages atτ=0.7\tau{=}0.7, group size88).(a)Early GRPO without intervention: tool usage drops from2.52.5to0by step77(reward hacking).(b)A no-penalty variant keeps tool calls but format stays low.(c)Under PARA-GRPO,κ\kappastabilizes at0.10.1–0.50.5whilefτf_{\tau}(green, right axis) climbs to0.410.41.

images.caption: Figure 9:Bidirectional tag reversion during RL.SFT with<tool_call>: RL also emits<tool_code>(3.63.6–3.9%3.9\%of rollouts). SFT with<tool_code>: RL still emits<tool_call>more often (5.4%5.4\%) than the SFT-trained tag (1.8%1.8\%). Tag substitution does not remove Format Fragility.
